description: Windows host compatibility projection baseline for Codex Desktop. Used by codex_state_audit.py and codex_state_repair.py; it is not the declarative source authority.
notes: Do not restore old config.toml files wholesale; merge only this baseline.

codex_baseline_update.py must merge verified current state into the existing baseline; it must not prune plugins, MCP servers, permissions, memory flags, or project trust just because they are absent from one live config snapshot.
Plugin cache presence is not proof of enablement; config and codex mcp list are the evidence.
After MCP server definitions change, restart Codex or refresh the MCP host.
Restored MCP expansion is verified by direct initialize/tools-list before config registration; do not remove these entries during repair.

Mobile OpenClaw bridge baseline as of 2026-06-19: CDP uses localhost:9229, not a hardcoded 127.0.0.1 assumption; OpenClaw final replies are successful only when Gateway /tools/invoke message/send returns ok=true with messageId; poll-once must wait for generationActive=false before returning final text.

Non-protected local stdio MCP launchers use _bridge/mcp_launch_guard.py wrappers; the guard serializes only the short prelaunch section and never holds a lifecycle lock for the MCP server. Stdio MCP processes are per client session and must not be reused across Codex sessions. Duplicate/orphan cleanup belongs to resource-process governance, not routine launch.

MCP stdio supervisors must preserve newline-delimited JSON-RPC framing. For local MCP servers in this workspace, proxy stdin/stdout line by line and flush each line; large blocking read(size) loops can delay short initialize/tools-call messages and create false tool-call timeouts.

Owned local stdio MCP fallback uses mcp-session tool-call with a fresh process and one bounded retry for transient initialize_response_missing or tool_call_response_missing. Do not retry real tool errors, schema mismatches, or missing commands.
CodeGraph MCP is registered through _bridge/mcp_profile_launcher.py profile cg; protocol smoke must exercise that launcher, not only the raw codegraph binary.

When CodeGraph wrapper smoke initializes and tools/list includes codegraph_explore but the active Codex turn still lacks mcp__codegraph, classify it as a current-session tool surface/binding issue and use the local codegraph-fallback for the current task. Do not treat it as a CodeGraph index/server failure or kill MCP processes.

MCP health must keep service_ok, session/tool-surface state, and current_turn_callable separate. A successful CLI/status/protocol smoke proves the MCP service can initialize, but does not prove the active Codex model turn exposes the tool. Current-turn tool failures must be recorded as current-codex-turn observations and surfaced as session binding faults, not service health.
Local PMB memory is the target unified memory body. Skills remain independent; legacy memory MCPs migrate into PMB and then leave the default MCP surface.

Unified memory entrypoint is C:\Users\45543\Desktop\Codex资源库\memory\memory_manifest.json; non-secret user profile partition is C:\Users\45543\Desktop\Codex资源库\memory\profiles\user_profile.json. Startup, workflow-gate, local_pmb_memory, and memory_governance validation must treat these files as part of memory availability, not optional documentation.
Local PMB memory uses a warm singleton daemon plus lightweight stdio proxy for Codex; do not replace the wrapper with direct pmb-mcp.exe or pmb mcp serve stdio.
PMB daemon duplicates are governed by local_pmb_memory doctor/repair-plan; proxy processes are per Codex session and are not the memory singleton.
After PMB cutover, codex_state_repair and codex_baseline_update must not restore decommissioned legacy memory MCPs.
Filesystem MCP is registered through _bridge/mcp_profile_launcher.py profile fs with bounded roots. It is installed for evaluation and not yet promoted as the default file-operation rule.

Filesystem admin MCP is Hub-managed with C:\ as its allowed root: reads use owner_mcp.call_readonly and authorized mutations use mcp_gateway.call under the existing filesystem-admin permission boundary; it must not be restored as an eager Desktop MCP.

Chrome DevTools, Playwright, GUI Automation, and Next AI Draw.io remain Desktop-native session-bound capabilities, but mcp_profile_launcher starts only mcp_lazy_stdio_proxy at task restore; the guarded heavy child starts on the first non-catalog call after command-fingerprinted catalog caches have been warmed.

Filesystem MCP usage is governed by _bridge\filesystem_mcp_policy.json and _bridge\docs\filesystem_mcp_usage_policy.md: codegraph remains primary for code understanding, rg remains primary for broad search, apply_patch remains primary for manual code edits, filesystem-admin is explicit high-scope access only.
Custom slash commands MCP is a local prompt-template registry only; it does not execute shell commands.

SQLite MCP defaults to the writable sqlite-scratch profile for agent working data. Production or business databases must be selected explicitly and should default to read-only unless the owning maintenance command authorizes a write path.

SQLite MCP is split into sqlite-scratch for dedicated scratch writes and sqlite-bridge-ro for read-only active bridge DB inspection; both use local Python sqlite3 to avoid heavy npm/native startup cost.

For complex SQLite writes, prefer sqlite_insert_record/sqlite_upsert_record or _bridge\tool_coordination.py record-* commands. Keep sqlite_execute for short bounded SQL only because long raw SQL plus many params can trip the current Codex MCP tool-surface dispatch/cancel path even when SQLite and the server are healthy.

Tool coordination between custom-slash-commands and sqlite-scratch is governed by _bridge\docs\tool_coordination_contract.md and _bridge\tool_coordination.py. This layer stores coordination metadata only and must not replace business-module source-of-truth state.

When Defender allowlisting is applied for a threat, the governance baseline must also clear the related residual history. If immediate deletion is access denied, it must schedule deletion on reboot and then re-check the threat state after restart rather than leaving the residual IsActive entry unaddressed.

When all expected MCPs are visible in codex mcp list and protocol smoke lists expected tools, but the active turn lacks the corresponding mcp__ tools, classify the root fault as Codex session/tool-surface drift, not an MCP service failure.

For current-thread tool-surface drift, run python _bridge\tool_exposure_doctor.py doctor --thread-id <thread_id>; stale thread cli_version plus empty thread_dynamic_tools is evidence for codex_thread_runtime_surface_drift. Do not hand-edit thread_dynamic_tools; use fallbacks for the current task and, if the same active turn still does not expose the tool surface after config/service health checks, use the approved controlled Codex Desktop restart/start-codex-desktop-elevated.ps1 path to force a fresh new-turn binding.

MCP stability requires a current-turn warmup/probe after config and protocol smoke: call the actual MCP tool in the active turn, record source=current-codex-turn tool_available/tool_unbound, and report full usability only from fresh active-turn evidence. Current-turn observations expire quickly and must not be reused as proof for later turns. Controlled rebind is capped at one attempt per incident; repeated failure must be investigated as session binding/startup drift, not looped restarts.

mcp-session doctor must keep stale current-turn negative observations as historical evidence but only treat fresh unsuperseded negatives inside the current-turn observation window as active risk. This prevents old Transport closed records from masking a later repaired tool layer.

If an exposed MCP namespace returns Transport closed, treat that profile as unavailable for the rest of the active turn, record a current-codex-turn negative observation, stop repeated direct calls, and check the whole tool layer for the same binding pattern. For owned local stdio MCPs, continue through mcp-session tool-call fresh-stdio fallback after protocol smoke confirms the server, because smoke alone is only health evidence and not task execution.

Bridge app-server idle restart is allowed only when the bridge queue is idle, supplement_waiting_mcp_ack is zero, no recent bridge delivery/recovery events exist, and the recent-activity probe succeeds. Probe failure fails closed. For protocol_violation_no_owned_result, check app-server owner churn and maintenance restart records before treating the fault as only a prompt/protocol output issue.

Mobile delivery ownership is based on durable receipt/outbox state and owned-result markers, not visible input or visible assistant text. Visible evidence is diagnostic only. Before retrying or redelivering a mobile task, check whether the old expected result code already appeared in thread history or bridge evidence.

MCP/tool stability governance must stay small and layered: expose config_ok, protocol_ok, current_turn_exposed, current_turn_callable, and call_completed separately; only call_completed is proof of active-turn usability, and app-server owner missing is a primary-route advisory until CDP/CLI fallback checks also fail.
MCP profiles previously launched through guarded-*.cmd now use _bridge/mcp_profile_launcher.py to avoid cmd.exe/conhost popup fanout while preserving mcp_launch_guard behavior.

WSL startup registers only the session-bound node_repl wrapper and the local MCP Hub. Hub-managed profiles such as custom slash commands, SQLite, filesystem, memory, and graph tools stay behind the Hub catalog instead of being duplicated as eager MCP registrations; Windows-only session capabilities remain in the host compatibility config. Runtime exceptions are exposed by smoke/probe/doctor instead of blocking old conversation recovery.
last_adopted_reason: Adopt stable browser plugin after WSL runtime authority migration
